title: One-Step Genotyping Protocol for Detection of Plasmodium falciparum hrp2 Gene Deletions
author: Djiby SOW
materials: Thaw PCR reagents (dNTP mix, primers, and Q5 Buffer) at room temperature except for enzymes. Vortex and microcentrifuge briefly and place in bench top cooler.

1.0 Equipment/Materials (if applicable) A B 1 Item Definition 2 Multichannel Pipettes Rainin LTS Pipet-Lite Multichannel series (or equivalent) 3 Single channel Pipettes Rainin LTS Pipet-Lite XLS Single channel series (or equivalent) 4 Centrifuge Bench top centrifuge with 96-well microwell plate rotor e.g. Sorvall Legend RT (or equivalent) 5 Thermocycler BioRad C1000 Thermal Cycler (or equivalent) 6 4°C Fridge Thermo Scientific Revco (or equivalent) 7 -20°C Freezer Kenmore (or equivalent) 1.0 Reagents and Media 1.1 Reagents: A B C 1 Material Name Other Information Storage Condition 2 Q5 5x Buffer -10°C to -30°C 3 Q5 High-fid enzyme -10°C to -30°C 4 dNTP Mix (10mM each) 10mM each -10°C to -30°C 5 Primers Forward and Reverse -10°C to -30°C 6 Nuclease Free Water Ambient Temperature 1.1 Primers: A B C D 1 Gene name Primer name Primer direction Primer sequence 2 pfhrp2 Bravo_f1 Forward 5’- ATGATTCATTATTCTATATTTATAAGGAAGATTAC-3’ 3 Bravo_r1 reverse 5’- CACTTCATGTATTTATGTATGCAGAAC- 3’
The negative control has a product band. 1.2.
Change pipette tip between each sample each time.
Briefly centrifuge (5-10 seconds) to collect all the reagents into the bottom of the tube. Bring the tubes or plate to PCR machine room. Load tubes or plate into the thermocycler machine.
Once agarose has solidified, gently remove the comb(s).

steps.title: This protocol provides a method for...
steps.instruction: This protocol provides a method for performing Plasmodium falciparum histidine-rich protein-2 (PfHRP2) genotyping on genomic DNA extracted from either dried blood spots (DBSs) on Whatman 903 filter paper or whole blood.

steps.title: This SOP applies to all clinical...
steps.instruction: This SOP applies to all clinical research and surveillance activities related to Plasmodium falciparum malaria. It is intended for use by technical personnel with a background in molecular biology and is strictly limited to research and surveillance purposes.

1.1 96-well PCR plate, half-skirt, stored at ambient temperature 1.2 MicroAmp Clear Adhesive Film, stored at ambient temperature 1.3 PCR workstations with UV light 1.4 Vortex 1.5 1.5 or 2.0 ml sterile RNAse and DNAse-free tubes 1.6 Tube racks 1.7 DNAse-free or sterile solution reservoirs or basins 1.8 Benchtop cooler or ice box 1.9 Microwave for agarose gel preparation 1.10 Standard top-load balance 1.11 Agarose gel electrophoresis apparatus and combs 1.12 Power supply for gel systems 1.13 Bioimage system or UV transilluminator 1.14 Biohazard bag with holder or plastic container for tip and tube disposal 1.15 Powder-free latex, vinyl or nitrile gloves 1.16 Kimwipes1.17 Permanent markers 1.18 Clean laboratory coat and protective goggles 1.19 70% ethanol in spray bottle

1.1 Wear personal protective equipment (PPE) at a minimum a lab coat, gloves andgoggles. 1.2 Adhere to established biosafety guidelines and follow recognized standards outlined in microbiological and biomedical laboratory safety manuals. 1.3 Universal precautions for working with infectious agents must be followed when performingthis assay. 1.4 PCR cabins and crosslinker are equipped with UV lights, cautions should be considered during decontamination. 1.5 Use aerosol-resistant pipette tips to minimize PCR contamination. 1.6 Decontaminate all surfaces and pipettes with 70% ethanol, before and after each use accordingto DPDM.DR.C.003, Decontamination of Work Area and Waste Material and use of Disinfectant. 1.7 Follow safety data sheets (SDS) when handling all chemicals including those known tobe carcinogenic and reproductively toxic.

steps.title: The positive controls are obtained from cultured P
steps.instruction: The positive controls are obtained from cultured P. falciparum strains, such as 3D7 or 7G8 (wild type), Dd2 (mutant- pfhrp2 deleted) stored in a laboratoryfreezer set to -20°C, or in a laboratory freezer set to -80°C for long-term storage. 1.1.

steps.title: Negative control is normal human DNA,...
steps.instruction: Negative control is normal human DNA, stored in a laboratory freezer set to -20°C. 1.1.

steps.title: Blank control well without DNA template...
steps.instruction: Blank control well without DNA template must be run with samples in the same plate. 1.1.

steps.title: Repeat run if control results are invalid
steps.instruction: Repeat run if control results are invalid. (See Section 11.
1.2 When required, the amplified DNAproducts will be confirmed by gel electrophoresis. PCR will be repeated if: 1.2.

steps.title: The major product is not the expected size
steps.instruction: The major product is not the expected size. 1.2.

steps.title: The major product is of the...
steps.instruction: The major product is of the correct size, but too much smearing exists. 1.2.

steps.title: There is no PCR product visible. NOTE
steps.instruction: There is no PCR product visible. NOTE: a negative result will occur for pfhrp2 -deleted samples. Following an initial negative pfhrp2 PCR result,the PCR should be repeated up to two more times. If negative for all 3reactions, WHO guidelines need to be followed to complete identification of pfhrp2 -deletion. 1.2.

steps.title: If re-amplification is not valid, DNA...
steps.instruction: If re-amplification is not valid, DNA re-extraction should be performed at leastonce.

steps.title: To prevent contamination with amplicons, preparation...
steps.instruction: To prevent contamination with amplicons, preparation of primers and master mix buffer must be performed in different and/or physically separated work areas. Follow the recommendations of the lab.

steps.title: Turn on the UV light in...
steps.instruction: Turn on the UV light in the PCR Workstation for 20 minutes (or as recommended by the manufacturer) before use.

steps.title: PCR master mix preparation should be...
steps.instruction: PCR master mix preparation should be performed on your lab bench space (unless otherwise advised).

steps.title: Remove enzyme from -20ºC freezer and...
steps.instruction: Remove enzyme from -20ºC freezer and leave on ice or bench top cooler.

steps.title: Prepare the master mix based on...
steps.instruction: Prepare the master mix based on the following amounts of reagents for a 50µl PCR reaction in 1.5 ml tube or solution basin. A B 1 Primary 2 Reagents µL/Sample 3 Q5 5x buffer (NEB) 10 4 dNTP mix (10mM) 1 5 Bravo_f1 (10µM) 0.63 6 Bravo_r1 (10µM) 0.63 7 Q5 High Fidelity Enzyme Mix 0.5 8 H20 32.24 9 10 Template DNA (add to tube separately) 5 11 12 Final reaction volume 50

steps.title: Mix and place the master tube or basin on ice
steps.instruction: Mix and place the master tube or basin on ice.

steps.title: Label 200µl thin-walled PCR tubes or...
steps.instruction: Label 200µl thin-walled PCR tubes or label a 96-well plate (according to testing format).

steps.title: Add 45µl of the master mix...
steps.instruction: Add 45µl of the master mix into each PCR tube or well of PCR plate.

steps.title: Take the PCR tubes (or plates)...
steps.instruction: Take the PCR tubes (or plates) with the mastermix to the PCR Workstation and add 5µl isolated DNA template into the corresponding tube or wells.

steps.title: Cap the tube or seal the PCR plate tightly
steps.instruction: Cap the tube or seal the PCR plate tightly.

steps.title: Select pre-programmed PCR protocol or set...
steps.instruction: Select pre-programmed PCR protocol or set up a new protocol based on the cycling profiles in the table below.

steps.title: Check the accuracy of the program...
steps.instruction: Check the accuracy of the program before pressing the run button. Only transfer to the thermocycler once 98°C has been reached. %s %s

steps.title: Proceed immediately to the Agarose gel...
steps.instruction: Proceed immediately to the Agarose gel preparation or place the PCR plate in the 4℃ refrigerator overnight. 2 Agarose gel preparation to confirm the PfHRP2 amplification results Note: All the gel electrophoresis steps should be performed in the gel room

steps.title: Prepare 1% (w/v) agarose solution
steps.instruction: Prepare 1% (w/v) agarose solution. Add 1.0 g agarose and 100 ml 1x TBE to a glass flask or bottle with a magnetic spin bar.

steps.title: Carefully microwave agarose gel solution until...
steps.instruction: Carefully microwave agarose gel solution until boiling (1 to 2 minutes at full power). Swirl using heat resistant glove to prevent heat injury. Repeat until agarose has completely dissolved into solution.

steps.title: Cool solution to 60°C on a magnetic plate spinner
steps.instruction: Cool solution to 60°C on a magnetic plate spinner.

steps.title: Add µl of GelRed (10,000x stock)...
steps.instruction: Add µl of GelRed (10,000x stock) to the 100 ml agarose solution for a 1x solution. Mix thoroughly.

steps.title: Set up gel-casting tray and comb...
steps.instruction: Set up gel-casting tray and comb and carefully pour agarose gel solution into the gel cast.

steps.title: Remove any bubbles and allow agarose...
steps.instruction: Remove any bubbles and allow agarose to solidify at room temperature (approximately 30 minutes).

steps.title: Place gel into electrophoresis apparatus with...
steps.instruction: Place gel into electrophoresis apparatus with sample wells closest to the negative (black) end of the gel chamber.

steps.title: Slowly pour 1x TBE running buffer...
steps.instruction: Slowly pour 1x TBE running buffer to the fill line of gel-running apparatus and ensure that the gel is completely submerged in the buffer.

steps.title: Add 2µl of 5x DNA loading...
steps.instruction: Add 2µl of 5x DNA loading dye into a new tube or on the surface of a parafilm strip for each sample to be run.

steps.title: Add 8µl of PCR product to...
steps.instruction: Add 8µl of PCR product to the loaded 5x dye and mix well.

steps.title: Load 8µl of the appropriate molecular...
steps.instruction: Load 8µl of the appropriate molecular marker in the first well

steps.title: Load 8µl of each sample to...
steps.instruction: Load 8µl of each sample to the wells; change pipette tip with each sample to prevent cross-contamination.

steps.title: Run the gel at 100V for...
steps.instruction: Run the gel at 100V for about 1 hour to migrate gel loading dye front and separate the DNA samples.

steps.title: Check to see that loaded material...
steps.instruction: Check to see that loaded material has run toward the positive (red) end of the gel box sufficiently to visualize the bands and identify specific sizes.

steps.title: Utilize the Bioimage system or UV...
steps.instruction: Utilize the Bioimage system or UV transilluminator to produce an image of the gel for recording.

steps.title: The expected secondary PCR band size...
steps.instruction: The expected secondary PCR band size is 1300 bp for in-tact pfhrp

steps.title: Please note, this size can vary...
steps.instruction: Please note, this size can vary in the instance of partial deletion.

steps.title: Jones S, Subramaniam G, Plucinski MM,...
steps.instruction: Jones S, Subramaniam G, Plucinski MM, Patel D, Padilla J, Aidoo M, Talundzic E. One-step PCR: A novel protocol for determination of pfhrp2 deletion status in Plasmodium falciparum. PLoS One. 2020 Jul 23;15(7):e0236369. doi: 10.1371/journal.pone.0236369. PMID: 32702040; PMCID: PMC7377462.
